action_required: Run /oracle-reconcile to sync oracle_sleeve.json with broker before Delphi can trade
note: Rotation plan computed but no orders placed due to pre_trade_check failure
Pre-trade check detected personal/pre-existing positions in broker overlapping with Delphi symbols. Sector orders confirmed in queued state. SPY overlay (~$29) deferred until reconciliation.
All sector positions within rebal band; SPY overlay queued GFD at ~$741.00. Pre-trade check flagged Oracle HDSN fill-price drift (0.51sh) — recommend /oracle-reconcile.

exit_reason_MU: New DRAM price-fixing class-action (w/ Samsung, SK Hynix) is a real overhang; -9.64% since entry, marginal MA break not offset by a clean noise explanation.
hold_reason_MRVL: Dip driven by Russell small-cap index removal (mechanical, market-cap graduation) not fundamentals; UBS PT raised to $340, Nvidia partnership intact, only 1 day since entry, -4.5% below MA.

exit_reason: Broad semiconductor-sector rotation (SOXX -7%+ intraday) driven by cautious AI-capex outlook from Broadcom, a deepening memory-chip price-fixing class action (Micron/Samsung/SK Hynix -- the same overhang that triggered yesterday's MU exit), and a rising-yield rotation out of crowded semis. SPY flat (+0.05%) confirms this is sector-specific, not market-wide noise, so the MA exit was honored for both names. MRVL's prior 1-day hold override (Russell index removal) was not renewed: reasoning was time-bound ('only 1 day since entry') and no longer applies on day 2 with the position now -10.5% below its MA.
note: Second Delphi pass today -- an earlier same-day run (07:xx UTC) had already exited MU and entered ELV. This run reviewed the book again under live quotes after a sharp intraday semis rotation and found two new MA-break exit candidates (LRCX, MRVL).
veto_reason_LRCX: Just exited this same run for a fundamental sector-rotation reason; immediately re-entering would contradict the exit thesis.
weight_override_reason: Three most-extended semis names (93-194% momentum) also hit hardest in today's sector selloff and sitting closest to MA breach (0.9-4.4% above); modest underweight reflects elevated near-term rotation risk while mechanical system still holds them.

note: Third hourly pass today. All 10 held positions remain above their 20-day MA (zero exit candidates). Cooldown-excluded top-10 momentum picks (INTC, AMD, AMAT, KLAC, UNH, CVS, ELV, CAT, GOOGL, BAC) are identical to current holdings -- LRCX, LLY, GE, C rank higher but are cooldown-locked from the prior two passes today. Max position drift (INTC, -18.1%) is within the 20% rebalance band. Zero orders placed.

exit_reason: AMD, INTC, KLAC are part of the same chip/semis-sector rotation flagged in the prior two runs today (SOXX selloff, memory price-fixing overhang) -- INTC -8.9% and KLAC -15.3% since entry, both already weight-tilted down last run for this exact risk. No new offsetting information; honored the mechanical exit for all three, consistent with the LRCX/MRVL exits earlier today.
hold_reason_CAT: Marginal breach, -0.35% below MA (essentially at the line). No CAT-specific negative catalyst identified; underlying momentum still strong (+43.8% 65-day). Distinct from the semis complex driving the other exits.
hold_reason_GOOGL: Bought this same morning (07-02) at 358.63; now 353.97, only -1.19% below its 20-day MA computed almost entirely from pre-ownership history. A few hours of intraday noise on a same-day entry is not a trend break.
note: Fourth Delphi pass today. Semis-sector rotation from earlier runs continued and deepened; exited AMD/INTC/KLAC on that established thesis. Held GOOGL (same-day entry noise) and CAT (marginal at-the-line breach, no catalyst) -- 2 holds, at the per-run cap. SQ (renamed XYZ) excluded from this pass's universe ranking due to a historicals fetch gap for the new ticker; not a held position, flagged for next universe-curation pass.
weight_override_reason: AMAT sits only 0.32% above its own MA and is the last remaining name directly in the chip-equipment complex after today's rotation; modest underweight reflects it being one bad session from becoming the next exit candidate.
